text: The surgeon marks the margins circumferentially around the rectal lesion, leaving about a centimeter from the lesion. A full thickness excision is performed up to the mesorectum, revealing the yellow mesorectal fat. The dissection continues circumferentially using scissors and fenestrated bipolar forceps, ensuring careful handling of the lesion. After the lesion is fully dissected, the area is washed, hemostasis is checked, and the defect is closed with a V-lock suture.
